name: Hartmans de Bont (HdB) minimal medium (PMC6794299)
description: Hartmans de Bont (HdB) minimal medium — medium reported in PMC6794299 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC6794299 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: For broth culture, M. smegmatis was grown on Hartmans de Bont minimal medium [67] supplemented with 0.05% (w/v) tyloxapol and 5.8 mM glycerol.

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 67 (Hartmans S, De Bont JAM 1992, Appl Environ Microbiol 58:1220-6, PMID 1599242, PMC195578)
sources_chased.how: primary is a scanned PDF (PMC195578, text not machine-readable); identical HdB recipe recovered verbatim from Frontiers 2020 (doi:10.3389/fbioe.2020.593781) and corroborated by web search of the primary
added: trace solution: EDTA, ZnSO4, CaCl2, FeSO4, Na2MoO4 (molybdenum), CuSO4, CoCl2, MnCl2

missing_before: molybdenum / molybdate (Na2MoO4) - present in HdB trace solution but absent from current_components (no EX_mobd/EX_mo)
explicit salt identities and concentrations for (NH4)2SO4, MgCl2, Na2HPO4, KH2PO4 and each trace metal (previously only listed as ion exchanges with no source-backed concentrations)
tyloxapol and glycerol previously in current_uncovered / unquantified
recuration.notes: HdB is a purely mineral (vitamin-free) medium; mycobacteria need no added vitamins, so none are absent. Trace-element concentrations are the stock values (per Frontiers restatement of Hartmans & de Bont); the stock is added at 10 mL/L, so concentration_mM is the final in-medium molar (100-fold dilution). Only real gap versus current_components is molybdate; Ni/Se/W/B are genuinely not in this recipe. Growth was aerobic (37 C, 200 rpm) despite target 'facultative' tag.
